title: 4chan
description: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

items.title: /news/ News: House advances bill barring sitting Congress members from buying stocks
items.content_text: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/22/hou se-congressional-stock-trading.html ?msockid=196670d327c565552f78675826 d66426 The U.S. House advanced legislation on Wednesday that would stop members of Congress from buying individual stocks while in office, taking the first meaningful action on an issue that has dogged lawmakers for years. The bill passed on a vote of 232-198, with more than a dozen Democrats joining Republicans in support. There is broad public support for a ban on members of Congress tra

items.title: /g/ Technology: It intriguing to me how much the generative AI artifacts in photos, video and so…
items.content_text: It intriguing to me how much the generative AI artifacts in photos, video and sound remind of the psychedelic experience. I first had this thought a decade ago when google deep dream was released but with the onset modern AI tools I've started noticing even more subtle similarities. You know the tell tale sign of AI generated voice? The kind of sharp, tensed up tone? That is identical to what all sounds sound like on LSD.

items.title: /v/ Video Games: A super underrated part of a lot of old games is how you can feel they were made…
items.content_text: A super underrated part of a lot of old games is how you can feel they were made based on what the devs thought was cool, instead of being made based on internet trends or market data It's pretty crazy how you had games with dozens of devs and a production budget that were still the unique brainchild of the devs more than "cream of the crop" """indie""" games today, a lot of times while playing recent games I can tell exactly which trends they're trying to cash in and it immediately sours me on

items.title: /g/ Technology: PoE drills?
items.content_text: I’m looking for a drill I can power over Ethernet and control from home assistant. The idea is that I’d be able to position the drill and then use a voice command with my Alexa to trigger an automation in home assistant that starts or stops the drill. Saves me the hassle of having to hit the trigger every time.

items.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Hector (Release date TBD)
items.content_text: >Hector (Noel Gugliemi) served his country as an Army Ranger in Iraq and Afghanistan. Now, all he wants is a quiet life in Los Angeles—running his auto repair shop, grilling in the backyard, and raising his family. But when a ruthless South American cartel affiliate rolls into the neighborhood aiming to claim the block through fear and violence, Hector’s peaceful retirement comes to a dead stop.
